subject: Re: [PATCH 1/2] mm: vmalloc: streamline vmalloc memory accounting

reviews.author: Uladzislau Rezki
reviews.summary: The reviewer suggested moving *_node_page_stat() to the end of vm_area_alloc_pages(), or invoking mod_node_page_state on high-order pages before splitting, to avoid looping over small pages afterwards.
reviews.raw_body:
Can we move *_node_page_stat() to the end of the vm_area_alloc_pages()?

Or mod_node_page_state in first place should be invoked on high-order
page before split(to avoid of looping over small pages afterword)?

I mean it would be good to place to the one solid place. If it is possible
of course.

--
Uladzislau Rezk

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a mistake in their patch and apologized for it, indicating no fix is planned as the issue was caught before compilation.
reviews.raw_body:
Good catch, my apologies. Serves me right for not compiling
incrementally.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about how vmalloc memory accounting handles pages from different nodes in the fallback paths (bulk allocator and one-by-one loop). They acknowledge that individual pages could be coming from different nodes, but suggest that accumulating until it differs and then flushing might be an overcomplication for the slow path. The author asks for feedback on whether this approach is worth implementing.
sentiment_signals: questioning the value of a proposed solution

reviews.author: Uladzislau Rezki
reviews.summary: Reviewer agreed with the original solution and explicitly stated that they agree with Andrew's decision to keep it, providing a Reviewed-by tag.
reviews.raw_body:
I see. I agree it is easier to keep original solution. I see that
Andrew took it, but just in case:

Reviewed-by: Uladzislau Rezki (Sony) <urezki@gmail.com>

--
Uladzislau Rezki
